title: Nutty Crispy Turkish Leek Stir-fry
description: This show-off turkish dish features leeks roasted to a crispy texture and paired with nuttiness for a delicious pescatarian-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Turkish Red Pepper Paste
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with store-bought tomato paste for a smoother texture

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Slice leeks and rinse under cold water.
Combine sliced leek, minced garlic, olive oil, salt, cumin seed, and red chili flakes in a bowl. Toss gently until evenly coated.
Place the leek mixture on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast for 20-25 minutes or until crispy and golden brown, shaking pan occasionally to ensure even browning.
While leeks are roasting, prepare a small sauce by mixing Turkish Red Pepper Paste into olive oil in a separate bowl until smooth.
In the last 10 minutes of leek cooking time, add chopped pistachios to the baking sheet. Toss gently to mix and ensure even roasting.
Once the leeks are done, remove them from oven. Immediately stir in the red pepper paste sauce, allowing it to meld with the leeks and pistachios for 2-3 minutes.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
